item.title: In July of 2010 we opened a video game center.
item.description:
This part of the building was used as a martial arts studio. Was vacant when we moved in and started work in April of 2010. I remember because I quit my other job on my 29th birthday. This location is in Augusta, GA.
Signage went up late almost 2 weeks after we opened.
The tape you see on the floors were for our measurements, spacing out TV's, electrical, walls, columns, and other objects before we actually had them.
12 years ago this was the start!
Sanded and painted for over 2 weeks. If I'd known we were going to cover it completely with posters I might not have taken so much time.
This area is the front counter. Where customers come to ask for different games or if they need assistance.
Half walls just separated the action from the front and rear of the shop.
Of course we had to add lights.
This area would have everything from Smash Bros to Rock Band, currently has a N64, several NES, and a couple of arcades.
Black glass TV stands were a terrible idea. After about a year of cleaning these every day I mounted most of our screens.
This was before we opened. PS3, Xbox 360, PC, and wii.
This was a huge upgrade and made cleaning up so much easier.
We regularly fill up this space. This is an old picture but we have a day care here today with over 30 people.
Every weekend we stayed open all night 8pm to 8am. From 2014 to early 2020 right before corona shut downs were all nights 2 times a week.
Dancing, rock band, and other movement games were so much fun.
It was very common to reach capacity.
Smash Bros was a staple game.
Years ago we had some guys come in with this PC. I felt like doing something for them so I gave them one of our old PC cases!
They installed the parts right then. Probably one of my best memories.
Concessions were always the best part of the job.
Private all nights were common. 30-60 players that could play on our systems or bring their own!
Cleaning and repairing game systems is a huge part of the business.
Korean BBQ and cookouts were also common. We'd feed 100-300 people at every event!
In the coming weeks/months we are moving.
Due to the size and limitations on parking and capacity we are moving to a size that's almost double our current space!
We are attaching ourselves to a branch of the YMCA but will have our own exit and entrance.
This space is 6000 sq ft and will be completely renovated before we move in.
I've brought on several partners including the YMCA. This does several things it allows me to step back into a support role. I get to supply equipment and be more of a silent partner than have to work at the counter.
I was ready to put everything in storage or sell it off a little at a time. Later this summer most of the current equipment along with some more arcades and table top type games will all be sent about 20 miles away to open under a different name. The YMCA along with myself and my partners have a vision of esports being part of the YMCA programs. This first location is basically a test run for that part. If this first location does well I look to open at least 3 others (all attached to YMCA locations though maybe not as big as this one) with in the first 2 years. We close on the sell of our current location in 1 week. The YMCA location should be ready to go by late August, early September. I am both excited to be stepping away from the daily operations and anxious, hoping that the new managers and employees can operate the business with as much care as I have over the last 12 years. I plan to have an album showing both our move and the new place being built.
item.dc:creator: levelupgc

item.title: The Goth Girl Cosplay Collection
item.description:
Death - Sandman
Raven - Teen Titans!
Shego - Kim Possible
Lady Dimitrescu - Resident Evil Village
Bowsette - From the internet's collective brains
Marceline/Marshall Lee - Adventure Time
And... just me being a demon... Obviously my true form...

item.title: THESE. WERE. MY. EVEREST.
item.description:
It took seven goddamn tries to get these right. Over-proofing, under-proofing, broken butter, melted butter, ALL of the things. Pastry chefs and France clearly hate happiness and fun. BUT
I DID the thing! Or, le thing.

item.description: Finished this digital painting yesterday, took 10 hours in total. Painting time-lapse: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ps0Qvdr--7Y
item.dc:creator: wickedness

item.title: How do I make my Lady famous?
item.description:
Every time I post my cat, Lady, she gets a lot of attention and people are always super supportive and loving and I love the community
I rescued Lady from a junkyard while I was at work one day and at first she was skidish of being around humans but eventually she warmed up very well, she is the first cat I have ever owned!
Lady seems to be quite short but her height definitely does not match her age. She was predicted to be about 2 or 3 years old!
item.dc:creator: YoloJesus42000

item.title: In 1958, Life Magazine invited Marilyn Monroe and photographer Richard Avedon to recreate images of five celebrated actresses of different eras.
item.description:
Marilyn Monroe as Theda Bara, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Jean Harlow, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Lillian Russell, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Marlene Dietrich, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Clara Bow, 1958

item.title: It's still good
item.dc:creator: MuffledGorillaViolence

item.title: The Return of the Meme (vol. 549)
item.description:
A small LOTR dump for today; I tried to do a larger dump but it wouldn't upload for some reason... anyway let's get this Lembas Bread. I know, a bite is enough to fill the stomach of a grown man, but Pippin ate half of a piece.
I'm actually reserving judgement on this. Either we will have a great thing and Silmarillion memes, or a horrible thing and Silmarillion memes AND memes about how bad it is.
Treebeard is my favorite character in common between the books and movies...
Actually a badass in his own right, as it turns out.
Fingolfin was a badass
Things I would do if I had kids. I actually got a specific hardcover version of The Hobbit back when Borders was closing down, for reading to my hypothetical children and to hand down the generations. *fart noise* I really wanted to be a dad. And I was excited that I would get to be one with my last ex, and her kid liked me and, well... nope, that didn't work. I've tried reading aloud to The Pippin, but he tends to chew on the book instead. Which I guess toddlers do...?
Gimli doesn't get the love he deserves
This happened to my friend who I introduced to LOTR. I can't remember her imgur tag.
Pippin tax! Survive today! Drink water! Take your meds! Survive today until tomorrow's red dawn!
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Fellas, It is ok if you need to be the little spoon
item.dc:creator: TheFolkman

item.title: The exact instant my son went from terrified of all animals - to loving them.
item.description: He was terrified of all animals - dogs especially. Because they love to chase things that run away. He would just about curl up in a foetal position if Sandy came into the room. One day he agreed to go with me to take Sandy for a walk. Part way along he agreed to take her lead. This was that moment. Now he loves all animals - dogs especially.
item.dc:creator: NuArcher

item.title: Just made it through probation at my IT job, here’s some IT memes to celebrate!
item.description:
Every time.
Thankfully haven’t had anyone THIS dumb, but they’re getting close…
Ha
Must be a unicorn, I always use the readme
Had me in the first half not gonna lie
Thankfully I’m still in the field but they might put me on the support desk soon -_-
Really is just more expensive LEGOs
I have fully told a user “hold on I need to google this”
The older users like to lie about this for some reason
If only they were happy little outages…happy for the guys working, not so happy for the IT guys getting yelled at by management :/
Aiming for the networking team by the end of the year, Fingers crossed
you can never pay me enough money to deal with printers only for the rest of my life, NEVER.
R.I.P internet explorer, you…won’t really be missed sorry buddy
Still not the worst rack I’ve seen
Teams, teams everywhere
Punpunpunpunpun
UNLIMITED POWAAHHH
Seriously
JPEG superiority
Or getting called over from across the building
WHY DO YOU LIE IM NOT THE POLICE
Pupper tax, just got him a month ago, lady said he was shitzu-chihuahua, vet papers say Shitzu-X, guess we’ll see what he looks like in a couple months
item.dc:creator: DanTheReactionMan
